name: How do I get my company actually mentioned in ChatGPT answers instead of my competitors?

mainEntity.name: How do I get my company actually mentioned in ChatGPT answers instead of my competitors?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You get mentioned in ChatGPT by publishing highly specific, answer-first content on your site, earning citations from trusted third‑party sites, and making your pages easy for AI and search crawlers to understand.
ChatGPT pulls from sources it already trusts, so you need both strong on‑site content and off‑site signals.

**Key factors**
- Clear, 2–3 sentence answers at the top of key pages.
- FAQ and HowTo style content that mirrors real questions.
- Clean technical setup: crawlable HTML, correct schema, fast site.
- Mentions and links from reputable sites, directories, and reviews.

Start by listing 15–20 real buyer questions, then create one best‑on‑the‑internet answer page for each and support it with citations from other sites and profiles.
Review ChatGPT answers to those questions monthly and refine your content based on which sources it cites.
Position this as an ongoing "visibility program," not a one‑time SEO trick.
acceptedAnswer.description: To get your company mentioned in ChatGPT, create very specific, answer‑first content around real buyer questions, make your site technically easy to crawl (HTML + schema), and earn mentions and links from trusted third‑party sites and directories. Then test prompts monthly and iterate based on which sources ChatGPT cites.
